items.prompt: An extra zero in blood pressure (Example 5.22) is best treated as:
choices: A reason to drop the entire column
items.explain: Typo-style outliers differ from legitimate clinical extremes (Example 5.23).

choices: Slow training and invite fitting noise
items.explain: Examples 5.29–5.32 list noise, interpretability, and runtime costs.

items.prompt: With 1% fraud positives, accuracy alone is a reliable metric.
items.explain: Example 5.33 shows majority-class accuracy can hide fraud detection failure.
Example 5.26 uses log transform to handle skew.
